summary: Use new sqlite runtime names instead of inventing new syntax: `foreign unsafe` plus `sqlite:unsafe*` was enough to make the boundary explicit across checker, contract emission, and runtime execution.
ergonomics: `runtime/bun/server.mjs` already had a solid sqlite contract shape, so attaching `unsafe` metadata and audit descriptors stayed local.
`test/Main.hs` is still the main friction point; adding one sqlite feature means editing distant source-fixture, checker, runtime-script, and assertion sections.
Focused runtime regressions were necessary here because the value is in emitted contract metadata and host behavior, not only typechecking.
follow_ups: Add an opt-in runtime hook to stream unsafe sqlite audit entries instead of only exposing `auditEntries()` polling.
Centralize sqlite runtime-name parsing/classification so checker and runtime evolution do not drift as more sqlite surfaces are added.
If safer SQL builders arrive later, document the split clearly so `sqlite:query*` and `sqlite:unsafe*` intent is obvious in examples.
warnings: `executeSqliteBinding` is top-level, so new sqlite behavior must route through runtime methods or other top-level helpers; nested helpers inside `createSqliteRuntime` are not directly callable there.
The checker currently enforces named-record row contracts on unsafe sqlite returns only; unsafe parameter validation was intentionally left aligned with existing runtime behavior.
Tests that assert audit counts should either clear the runtime audit log or create a fresh sqlite runtime instance first.
tests_run: nix develop -c bash -lc 'cabal test clasp-compiler-test --test-options="-p \"sqlite runtime exposes explicit unsafe SQL bindings with row-contract metadata and audit entries\""'
nix develop -c bash -lc 'cabal test clasp-compiler-test --test-options="-p \"unsafe sqlite\""'
nix develop -c bash -lc 'cabal test clasp-compiler-test --test-options="-p \"rejects sqlite unsafe bindings\""'
residual_risks: Unsafe sqlite audit entries are runtime-local and in-memory; they expose metadata and SQL text for host inspection but are not yet streamed to an external audit sink.
Only explicit `sqlite:unsafe*` bindings emit these audit entries; direct helper calls like `queryOne`/`mutateOne` remain ordinary runtime helpers without unsafe-audit metadata.
